data.content:
# Implementation Plan — Preserve tag casing

## Diagnosis

Tag casing is lost in three places, all by `to_ascii_lowercase()`:

1. **Write path (CLI -> events)**
   - `src/app/helpers.rs::normalize_tag` (line 119) — used by `knot_create` (line 262) and `knot_update::fields::collect_tags` (lines 211, 227). Lowercases before emitting `KnotTagAdd`/`KnotTagRemove` events.
2. **Rehydrate path (events -> projection)**
   - `src/app/rehydrate/apply_event.rs::apply_tag_add` (line 240) and `apply_tag_remove` (line 249) — lowercases the tag value pulled out of the event payload.
3. **Sync ingest path (events -> cache from remote)**
   - `src/sync/apply.rs::apply_tag_add` (line 395) and `apply_tag_remove` (line 414) — same, on the sync side.

Filter side already case-folds at `src/listing.rs::has_all_tags` (line 137) and `normalize_scalar` (line 167), so listing is case-insensitive today.

Tags are stored as a JSON array on `knot_hot.tags_json` (no DB index, no FTS over tags). Events are immutable; lowercased historical events stay lowercased forever.

## Approach

Switch storage to preserve the user-supplied casing, and switch all dedup/removal/match comparisons to case-insensitive (`eq_ignore_ascii_case`). No data migration required: old projects keep their lowercased event payloads, but their listings still match mixed-case queries because the filter already lowercases both sides.

### Changes

1. `src/app/helpers.rs::normalize_tag` — drop `.to_ascii_lowercase()`, keep `.trim()`.
2. `src/app/knot_create.rs` (around line 266) — replace `tags.iter().any(|t| t == &normalized)` with `eq_ignore_ascii_case`.
3. `src/app/knot_update/fields.rs::collect_tags` — `eq_ignore_ascii_case` for both add-dedup (line 215) and remove-match (lines 231-232).
4. `src/app/rehydrate/apply_event.rs::apply_tag_add` / `apply_tag_remove` — trim only; existence/retain checks use `eq_ignore_ascii_case`.
5. `src/sync/apply.rs::apply_tag_add` / `apply_tag_remove` — same as above.
6. `src/listing.rs` — no structural change; existing case-folding preserves backward compat.

### Tests

- Update `src/app/tests_error_paths.rs` line 71: `normalize_tag("  A-B  ")` -> `"A-B"`.
- New test: creating a knot with `Journey-Github-Connect` stores the exact string.
- New test: `--add-tag MixedCase-Tag` preserves case; second add of `mixedcase-tag` is a no-op; remove of `MIXEDCASE-TAG` removes the tag.
- New listing test: stored mixed-case tag matched by mixed-case AND lowercase filter.
- Backward-compat listing test: stored lowercase tag matched by mixed-case filter.
- Sync test: an `EventKnotTagAdd` event with `Journey-Github-Connect` produces a cache row whose tags contain that exact string.

Add new tests to `listing_tests_ext.rs` since `listing.rs` is at 499 lines (file-size limit is 500).

## Backward Compatibility

- Existing event logs untouched. Tags rehydrated from old events remain lowercase (immutable history).
- Existing queries against old data unchanged (filter is case-insensitive).
- No schema/migration changes required.

## Risks

- `normalize_tag` is used only in the listed call sites (verified via grep).
- If a knot already has `journey-github-connect` and the user adds `Journey-Github-Connect`, the case-insensitive dedup keeps the original (lowercase) entry — preserves immutability of old data, avoids remove+add churn.
- Coverage gate (95%) — every new branch needs a test.
- File-size limit (500 lines) — listing.rs sits right at the edge; new tests go to `listing_tests_ext.rs`.

## Complexity

Small. ~5 single-line edits, ~5 new tests, 1 updated assertion. No new abstractions.

## Verification

1. `make sanity` (fmt, lint, tests, coverage).
2. Manual smoke against a scratch project per the knot's verification steps 1-5.
